snapshot.title: Phase 1.4: Create Database Migration Script
snapshot.description:
Create the actual Prisma migration and data migration scripts for the multi-campus schema changes.

Depends on @task-5, @task-6, @task-7 (All schema changes must be defined first).
See @doc/migrations/multi-campus-migration for context.

## Migration Steps

### 1. Pre-Migration Data Backup
- Document existing data counts for verification
- Create backup of current database state

### 2. Create Default Campus
- Insert a default campus record for existing data
- This campus will receive all legacy data

### 3. Data Migration Script
Create SQL migration script that:
1. Creates all new tables (campus, permission, role_permission, staff_type)
2. Adds new columns to existing tables
3. Migrates staff_type enum values to staff_type table records
4. Assigns default campus_id to all existing records
5. Updates unique constraints (drop old, create new)
6. Removes deprecated columns (guardian.spouse_id, staff.staff_type text)

### 4. Seed Permissions
- Create seed script for initial permission records
- Permissions should match codebase module structure

### 5. Verification Queries
- Count records before/after migration
- Verify FK integrity
- Test unique constraint enforcement

## Files to Create/Modify
- prisma/migrations/[timestamp]_multi_campus_migration/migration.sql
- prisma/seeds/seed-campus.ts
- prisma/seeds/seed-permissions.ts
- prisma/seeds/seed-staff-types.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: Default campus created in migration
All existing staff records assigned to default campus
All existing guardian records assigned to default campus
All existing student records assigned to default campus
All academic entities assigned to default campus
All content entities assigned to default campus
staff_type enum values migrated to staff_type table
Old unique constraints dropped and new ones created
Deprecated columns removed (spouse_id, staff_type text)
Migration runs successfully: npx prisma migrate dev
Seed scripts created for campus, permissions, staff_types
Prisma client regenerated: npx prisma generate

changes.newValue:
1. Run npx prisma migrate dev --name profile_academic_content_campus_scoping
2. Verify migration SQL generated correctly
3. Update seed.ts:
   - Create default campus record for seed data
   - Add campusId to all Guardian seed records
   - Add campusId to all Student seed records
   - Remove spouse linking code (spouseId removed)
4. Run npx prisma generate to regenerate client
5. Run seed to verify: npx prisma db seed
6. Verify all foreign keys and indexes created

## Note: Simplified Migration (Empty Database)

The database is currently empty and fresh, which significantly simplifies this task:
- Skip: Pre-migration data backup (no data to backup)
- Skip: Data migration script for existing records (no existing data)
- Skip: Assigning default campus_id to legacy data (no legacy data)
- Skip: Migrating staff_type enum values (no existing staff records)

Focus only on:
1. Creating the Prisma migration with schema changes
2. Creating seed scripts for initial data (campus, permissions, staff_types)
3. Running prisma migrate dev and prisma generate

The acceptance criteria #2-#9 (related to migrating existing data) are auto-satisfied since there's no existing data to migrate.

## Migration Command

User needs to run manually (interactive mode required):
```bash
npx prisma migrate dev --name profile_academic_content_campus_scoping
```

## Schema Changes Summary
Migration SQL generated successfully with all the following changes:
- Added campus_id to: student, staff, guardian, grade_level, subject, school_year, class, student_attendance, post, post_audience, file
- Removed: spouse_id from guardian, staff_type text from staff
- Added: staff_type_id FK to staff
- Updated unique constraints to be campus-scoped
- Added indexes on campus_id for all affected tables

## Seed.ts Status
Left unchanged per user request. Will need updates to include campusId for:
- Guardian records (also remove spouseId linking)
- Student records
- Role records may need default campus or system default flag
